description_long: This church features a steeply pitched, dark slate roof with a prominent central bell tower topped by an ornate spire. The exterior walls are finished in a light, off-white plaster with tall, arched windows that allow natural light into the interior. The bell tower includes a clock face and is capped with a bulbous, Baroque-style dome. The main entrance is marked by a wooden door with a small overhang, and additional side entrances are visible. The structure is rectangular with a slightly protruding chancel and symmetrical window placement. Architectural details such as the window arches, roof shingles, and tower ornamentation suggest a traditional European design, likely Baroque or late Gothic Revival.
description: A traditional European church with a steep slate roof, central bell tower, arched windows, and light plastered walls, featuring ornate Baroque-style details.
description_view_0: This view highlights the main facade with the central bell tower, clock face, and the primary entrance beneath the steeply pitched roof.
description_view_1: This angle shows the side elevation with tall arched windows, a secondary entrance, and the full height of the bell tower and spire.
description_view_2: The rear and side of the church are visible, displaying additional arched windows and the protruding chancel area.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the long, uninterrupted roofline and the side wall with minimal window openings, as well as the rear of the bell tower.
